Overview

What is MBET?

Multicurrency Budget & Expense Tracker (MBET) is a personal-use Android app for managing monthly finances in multiple currencies. No account needed. No cloud. Your data stays on your device.

Multicurrency Budget & Expense Tracker

📅

Monthly Budget View

Each month is an independent workspace — track income and expenses per period with a named label.

💱

Multi-currency

Set a base currency and track transactions in any currency. Manual or live FX rates supported.

🌙

Dark & Light Theme

Toggle between dark and light mode any time. Theme is remembered between sessions.

📤

Export & Backup

Save full data backups to your device as JSON — anytime, no internet required.

🔒

Offline First

Core features work entirely without internet. No tracking, no server, no sign-in.

⭐

Free + Pro

Full budget tracking is free. Unlock Pro for savings goals, FX rates, interest tools and more.

Getting Started

First-time setup

No sign-up, no configuration. Install and start tracking in under a minute.

Install the APK

Download and install the signed APK. You may need to allow installation from unknown sources in Android Settings → Security if installing outside the Play Store.

Set your base currency

On first launch open the currency selector and pick your home currency. This drives all summaries and dashboard totals. You can change it anytime.

Name your first month

Tap the month label field and rename it (e.g. January 2026). This name appears in exports, summaries, and printed reports.

Add income entries

Tap + Add Income to create your first income item — salary, freelance, or any source. Give it a name, amount, and currency.

Add expense entries

Tap + Add Expense to log costs. Each entry can have its own currency, notes, and due date. Collapse entries to keep the screen tidy.

Review your dashboard

The dashboard shows total income, expenses, balance, and a per-currency breakdown — all updating in real time as you type.

Features

Core features

All features below are available to every user — no unlock required.

📋

Income Tracking

Multiple income sources per month. Each entry is individually named, collapsible, and supports any currency.

💸

Expense Tracking

Log expenses with names, amounts, currencies, and optional notes. Expand or collapse entries freely.

📊

Live Dashboard

Real-time income vs expenses summary with net balance and per-currency breakdown. Updates as you type.

💱

Multi-currency FX

Each entry can use any currency. Manual exchange rates convert everything to your base currency in the summary.

📆

Month Navigation

Switch between months with the month selector. Each month is stored independently.

➡️

Copy to Next Month

Carry your current month's income and expense entries forward to the next period in one tap.

🖨️

Print Compact Report

Opens the Android native Print / Save as PDF dialog with a formatted compact budget report for the active month.

💾

Save Summary

Export a plain-text summary of the active month — income, expenses, balance, and notes — as a .txt file.

☰

Mobile Drawer Menu

A slide-out hamburger menu gives quick access to export, import, reset, print, and theme toggle.

🔄

Reset Data

Clear all budget data and return the app to its initial state. Always export a backup first — this is irreversible.

Data Management

Export, Import & Backup

Your data never leaves your device unless you export it. Here is how each data action works.

Action What it does Format Status
📤 Export Data Saves all budget data (all months) as JSON. Updates the Last Export timestamp on the backup banner. JSON Free
🗂️ Export JSON Backup Saves a full backup to the app's data folder on device and shows the saved file location as an alert. JSON Free
📥 Import Data Load a previously exported JSON file. Overwrites current data. Includes a retry-safe mechanism — if it fails, try again. JSON Free
📝 Save Summary Exports a plain-text summary of the active month — income, expenses, balance, and notes. TXT Free
🗑️ Reset Data Permanently clears all months and entries. Returns the app to its blank initial state. Cannot be undone. — Irreversible
Tip: Exported files are saved to Android/data/com.mirodigitaltools.budgettracker/files/. Use any file manager to access or copy them.
Before you Reset: Always export a JSON backup first. Reset Data wipes all months and entries permanently and cannot be undone.
Pro Features

Unlock Pro

Power-user features available with a one-time Pro unlock. Core budget tracking always remains free.

⭐ PRO

Savings Goal Tracker

Due-Date Reminders

Assign due dates to expense entries and see visual reminders when payments are approaching.

⭐ PRO
⚡

Live FX Mode

Automatically fetch live exchange rates instead of entering them manually. Keeps multi-currency totals accurate.

⭐ PRO
📈

Interest Helper

Enable interest calculation on individual expense items — useful for loans, credit cards, or instalment plans.

Note: Pro status is stored locally. If you uninstall or reset the app, you may need to re-enter your unlock code.

FAQ

Frequently asked questions

Is my data stored online or in the cloud?
No. All budget data is stored locally on your Android device. Nothing is sent to any server. Data only leaves your device when you manually export it yourself.
How do I transfer my data to a new phone?
Use Export JSON Backup to save a backup file, copy that file to your new phone, then use Import Data to restore it. The file is saved in Android/data/com.mirodigitaltools.budgettracker/files/.
Can I use the app without an internet connection?
Yes — all core features work fully offline. The only feature requiring internet is Live FX Mode (Pro), which fetches live exchange rates. Manual FX rates work completely offline.
What happens to my data after a reset?
Reset Data permanently clears all months, all entries, and all settings. It cannot be undone. Always export a JSON backup before resetting — it can be re-imported immediately after.
Import isn't working — what should I do?
Make sure you are selecting a valid .json file exported from this app. The import flow includes a retry-safe mechanism — if the first attempt fails, tap Import again. This is especially relevant right after using Reset Data.
Print opens a share sheet instead of the print dialog.
This happens with older app versions. The current stable build uses the native Android print dialog. Uninstall the old version first, then reinstall the latest APK to clear any cached behavior.
How do I add a new month?
Use Copy to Next Month to create the next period with your current entries pre-filled, or navigate forward in the month selector — the app will create a new month automatically if one doesn't exist yet.
Can I track multiple currencies in the same month?
Yes. Each income and expense entry can use its own currency. Set your base currency, configure exchange rates (manually or via Live FX Pro), and the dashboard converts everything for the summary totals.
